definition: Section 80-IAC of the Income Tax Act grants a 100% deduction on profits for 3 consecutive years out of the first 10 years for DPIIT-recognised startups.
india_note: Apply via the Startup India portal after DPIIT recognition. Requires innovation and incorporation after April 1, 2016.

definition: Alternative Investment Fund — SEBI-registered private investment vehicles (VC funds, hedge funds, PE funds) that pool capital from sophisticated investors.
india_note: Category I AIFs include VC funds. The Fund of Funds for Startups (FFS) by SIDBI invests in India-registered AIFs.

definition: Informal term for Section 56(2)(viib) of Income Tax Act — a provision that taxes share premium above Fair Market Value (FMV) as 'income from other sources' in the hands of the startup.
india_note: DPIIT-recognised startups are exempt from angel tax if they file Form 2. Critical to get DPIIT recognition before your angel round.

definition: Annual Recurring Revenue — annualised value of all active subscription contracts. ARR = MRR × 12. The primary health metric for SaaS businesses.
india_note: Indian SaaS companies often report ARR in USD for global investor benchmarking, even when billing in INR.

definition: Building a company without external funding, using personal savings, revenue, or loans. Retains 100% equity but limits growth speed.
india_note: India has world-class bootstrap success stories — Zoho ($1B+ revenue, zero external funding), Zerodha (profitable unicorn).

definition: The monthly rate at which a company spends its cash reserves. Net burn = cash out minus cash in (revenue). Used to calculate runway.
india_note: Typical seed-stage burn in India: ₹20-50 lakh/month. Series A: ₹1-5 crore/month. Always model 18-24 month runway post-raise.

definition: Customer Acquisition Cost — total sales and marketing spend divided by number of new customers acquired in a period.
india_note: India benchmarks vary widely: D2C CAC ₹300-1,000, SaaS SMB CAC ₹5,000-50,000, enterprise SaaS ₹1,00,000+.

definition: Capitalization Table — a spreadsheet showing every shareholder, their shares, ownership %, and share class. The official record of who owns the company.
india_note: In India, cap tables must reflect MCA registered shareholding. Post-conversion of CCDs/convertible notes, ensure filings updated with MCA within 30 days.

definition: India's primary credit score (300-900) computed by TransUnion CIBIL. Reflects an individual's creditworthiness based on repayment history.
india_note: CRED restricts membership to 750+ CIBIL score. Founders with low CIBIL scores may face challenges getting business loans from banks.

definition: The period before any vesting occurs. A 1-year cliff means zero shares vest in the first year; after 12 months, 25% vests at once.
india_note: Standard Indian startup vesting: 4-year vesting with 1-year cliff. Critical to have vesting agreements registered with MCA.

definition: Short-term debt that converts to equity at the next funding round. Includes interest rate (typically 8%) and valuation cap or discount.
india_note: In India, use CCDs (Compulsorily Convertible Debentures) instead of US-style convertible notes — they are FEMA/RBI-compliant for foreign investors.

definition: Compulsorily Convertible Debenture — Indian equivalent of convertible notes. Must convert to equity within the instrument's tenure. Widely used for angel rounds and bridge rounds.
india_note: Preferred over simple convertible notes for FEMA compliance when taking foreign investment. File with MCA within 30 days of issue.

definition: Direct to Consumer — business model where brands sell products directly to consumers via own website or app, bypassing distributors/retailers.
india_note: India's D2C market is ₹4 lakh crore+. Shopify, Instagram, and WhatsApp Business are the primary D2C stack in India.

definition: Discounted Cash Flow — intrinsic valuation method that estimates company value based on projected future free cash flows, discounted back to present value.
india_note: DCF rarely used for early-stage Indian startups (no reliable cash flows). Comparables (revenue multiples, GMV multiples) are more common pre-revenue.

definition: Framework coined by Kunal Shah (CRED/FreeCharge) — a product creates a behavior change only when it is at least 4x more efficient than the incumbent solution.
india_note: Used widely in India investor circles to evaluate consumer tech ideas. CRED's thesis: paying credit card bill via app vs. bank website is a Delta 4 improvement.

definition: Reduction in existing shareholders' ownership percentage when new shares are issued (in a funding round, ESOP grants, or conversion of instruments).
india_note: Typical dilution per round: Seed 10-20%, Series A 15-25%, Series B 15-20%. Calculate pro-forma cap table before accepting any term sheet.

definition: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade — the Ministry that runs Startup India, grants DPIIT recognition, and administers schemes like SISFS and CGSS.
india_note: DPIIT recognition is the foundational step for every Indian startup. Unlocks tax exemptions, seed funds, fast-track patents, and GeM access.

definition: Draft Red Herring Prospectus — the public document filed with SEBI before an IPO, containing detailed financial information and risk factors.
india_note: DRHP is filed with SEBI and available publicly on SEBI website within 10 days of filing. Studying competitor DRHPs gives real unit economics data.

definition: Earnings Before Interest, Tax, Depreciation, and Amortization — a proxy for operating profitability. Shows recurring cash generation before financing and accounting.
india_note: Indian investors often cite 'EBITDA positive' as a milestone. Many Series B startups target EBITDA breakeven within 18-24 months of the round.

definition: Employee Stock Option Plan — the mechanism to grant company equity to employees. Options vest over time, exercisable at a predetermined strike price.
india_note: ESOPs taxed in India at two points: (1) on exercise (as perquisite/salary income) and (2) on sale (as capital gains). DPIIT startups can defer perquisite tax to sale.

definition: Foreign Exchange Management Act (1999) — governs foreign exchange transactions in India, including foreign investment in Indian startups.
india_note: Under FEMA, foreign VC investments in Indian startups require filings (FCGPR form) with RBI via AD bank within 30 days of receipt of funds.

definition: Foreign Direct Investment — investment from a foreign entity/person into an Indian company, governed by FEMA and FDI Policy.
india_note: Most startup sectors permit 100% FDI under automatic route (no government approval needed). EdTech, certain FinTech, and defence have sector caps.

definition: In India context: Farmer Producer Organization — a collective of farmers formed as a company under Companies Act to aggregate production and market collectively.
india_note: Government is forming 10,000 FPOs. AgriTech startups partner with FPOs for last-mile distribution and farmer data access.

definition: Government e-Marketplace — the official procurement portal for all central government ministries and PSUs. Annual procurement: ₹2+ lakh crore.
india_note: DPIIT-recognised startups get relaxed eligibility on GeM (no prior turnover requirement). A massive B2G market opportunity.

definition: Gross Merchandise Value — total value of goods transacted on a marketplace before deducting marketplace take-rate/commission. Not revenue.
india_note: Indian unicorns are often valued on GMV multiples (1-4x GMV for marketplaces). GMV ≠ Revenue. Always clarify which one when reading news.

definition: Revenue minus Cost of Goods Sold (COGS), expressed as a percentage. The most important unit economics metric for assessing business quality.
india_note: India benchmarks: SaaS 70-80% gross margin. D2C products 40-60%. Marketplaces 30-50% net revenue margin. E-commerce 20-35%.

definition: The classic startup growth curve — slow initial growth followed by a rapid, steep upward inflection (looks like a hockey stick lying on its side).
india_note: Investors look for this in metrics decks. Zomato and Swiggy showed hockey stick growth post-Jio in 2017. COVID was a hockey stick for EdTech.

definition: Indian Simple Agreement for Future Equity — India's adaptation of Y Combinator's SAFE note, created by 100X.VC. FEMA-compliant convertible instrument for angel rounds.
india_note: 100X.VC standardised iSAFE in 2018. Simpler and faster than CCDs. Used by 300+ Indian startups. Download template at 100x.vc.

definition: Initial Public Offering — the process by which a private company first sells shares to the public and lists on a stock exchange.
india_note: Indian IPOs listed on BSE/NSE. SEBI is regulator. Indian startup IPO track: Zomato (2021), Nykaa (2021), PolicyBazaar (2021), Mamaearth (2023), Ola Electric (2024).

definition: Investment performance curve showing initial negative returns (burn period) before returns turn positive with scale.
india_note: Used to describe VC fund performance. Also describes startup trajectory: burn in early years → profitability at scale. Explains why VCs have 10-year fund life.

definition: Limited Liability Partnership — an Indian business entity that combines partnership flexibility with limited liability protection. Popular for service businesses.
india_note: LLPs are DPIIT-recognisable for Startup India. But foreign VC investment into LLP has FEMA complications — prefer Private Limited for VC-backed startups.

definition: Lifetime Value — predicted total revenue from a customer over their entire relationship with the company. LTV:CAC ratio > 3x is a healthy unit economics benchmark.
india_note: India median LTV:CAC ratios: SaaS 3-5x, D2C 2-3x, FinTech 5-10x. Series A investors expect LTV:CAC > 3x with clear payback period.

definition: The right of preferred shareholders (investors) to be paid first (at 1x or higher their investment) before common shareholders in a liquidation or M&A event.
india_note: Participating preferred (double dip) vs. non-participating (1x liquidation then convert) — Indian term sheets often have 1x non-participating preference.

definition: Ministry of Corporate Affairs — governs company registration, compliance, and filings in India. MCA21 portal is where all company filings are submitted.
india_note: Every Indian Private Limited Company must file annual returns (MGT-7, AOC-4) and financial statements with MCA. Penalties for non-compliance are steep.

definition: Monthly Recurring Revenue — total predictable revenue generated in one month from subscriptions. The gold standard SaaS health metric.
india_note: Indian SaaS benchmark: ₹1 crore MRR (~$120K) is a key milestone for Series A readiness. ₹10 crore MRR targets Series B.

definition: Minimum Viable Product — the simplest version of a product with just enough features to test core hypotheses with early adopters.
india_note: WhatsApp groups, Google Forms, and Notion pages are classic Indian startup MVPs. Many successful startups validated with zero-code MVPs.

definition: Non-Banking Financial Company — RBI-regulated entity that provides financial services (lending, investment) but does not have a banking licence.
india_note: Many FinTech startups (Slice, KreditBee, Oxyzo) operate as NBFCs. Requires minimum ₹10 crore net owned fund and RBI registration. Takes 12-18 months.

definition: Non-Disclosure Agreement — legal contract preventing parties from sharing confidential information. Also called a Confidentiality Agreement.
india_note: Indian courts enforce NDAs under Contract Act 1872. Template NDAs from Startup India legal library. Most VC investors refuse to sign NDAs.

definition: Net Promoter Score — customer loyalty metric based on question 'How likely are you to recommend us to a friend?' (0-10). NPS = % Promoters (9-10) - % Detractors (0-6).
india_note: Indian B2C benchmarks: NPS 30+ is good. SaaS NPS 40+ for enterprise. Zomato, Swiggy typically NPS 20-35. Use surveys after every 3rd order.

definition: Net Revenue Retention — measures revenue retained from existing customers including expansions, upsells minus churns and downgrades. NRR > 100% means customers spend more over time.
india_note: NRR > 120% is the gold standard for SaaS. Freshworks listed at 130% NRR. Indian SaaS investors heavily weight NRR over ARR growth.

definition: Profit After Tax — the bottom line; net profit remaining after all expenses, depreciation, interest, and taxes.
india_note: PAT positive is a major milestone for Indian startups seeking IPO. SEBI requires profitability track record or exceptional cases under SME segment.

definition: A fundamental change in a startup's product, business model, target market, or strategy while retaining the same team.
india_note: Famous India pivots: Meesho (from Fashnear to social commerce), IndiaMart (B2B marketplace pivoted to SaaS), Practo (from clinic management software to consumer health app).

definition: Product-Led Growth — strategy where the product itself drives user acquisition, retention, and expansion without heavy sales force. Freemium is classic PLG.
india_note: Freshdesk/Freshworks pioneered PLG from India. Postman (10M+ users, no sales team initially). Works best for developer tools and B2B productivity apps.

definition: Product-Market Fit — the state where your product satisfies a strong market demand. Marc Andreessen: 'you can feel PMF when it's happening — customers pull the product from you.'
india_note: Indian PMF signals: organic word-of-mouth growth, repeat usage without marketing spend, customers angry when you talk about shutting down.

definition: Company valuation immediately after a funding round. Post-money = Pre-money + Investment Amount.
india_note: Used in all Indian term sheets. Example: ₹100 Cr pre-money + ₹25 Cr investment = ₹125 Cr post-money. Dilution = ₹25/₹125 = 20%.

definition: Company valuation immediately before a funding round. Agreed between founder and investor as the basis for calculating dilution.
india_note: At Seed: ₹5-25 Cr pre-money typical. Series A: ₹50-300 Cr. Series B: ₹300 Cr - ₹1,000 Cr. Valuation driven by team, traction, and market size.

definition: Investor's right to participate in future rounds to maintain their ownership percentage by investing their proportional share of the round.
india_note: Typically granted to investors owning >2% in Indian deals. Blume Ventures, Accel are known to exercise pro-rata rights actively in winners.

definition: Reserve Bank of India — India's central bank and monetary authority. Regulates banks, NBFCs, payment systems, and foreign exchange.
india_note: Critical for FinTech startups — RBI regulates payment aggregators, lending, prepaid instruments, and UPI. RBI sandbox available for FinTech innovation.

definition: Real Estate Regulatory Authority — state-level authority established under RERA Act 2016 to regulate real estate projects and protect homebuyers.
india_note: PropTech startups must understand RERA compliance. MahaRERA (Maharashtra), Karnataka RERA are most developed. Project registration, escrow mandatory.

definition: Number of months a startup can operate before running out of cash, at current burn rate. Runway = Cash / Monthly Net Burn.
india_note: Rule of thumb: raise when you have 6+ months runway remaining. Raise enough for 18-24 months. Never let runway drop below 6 months without a plan.

definition: Simple Agreement for Future Equity — a Y Combinator-created instrument that converts to equity at a future priced round, with a valuation cap and/or discount.
india_note: US SAFE is not FEMA-compliant in India. Use iSAFE (by 100X.VC) or CCDs for Indian/foreign investors. SAFEs are standard for YC-backed Indian companies raising from US.

definition: Serviceable Addressable Market — the portion of TAM your product can actually serve given your business model, geography, and segment focus.
india_note: SAM must be geographically and demographically honest. An EdTech for JEE preparation has TAM of 'India education market' but SAM of '700,000 JEE aspirants annually'.

definition: Securities and Exchange Board of India — regulates India's capital markets, stock exchanges (BSE/NSE), mutual funds, and Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs).
india_note: SEBI regulates VC funds (AIFs), crowdfunding platforms, and public companies. Startup IPO regulated by SEBI ICDR Regulations.

definition: Small Industries Development Bank of India — the apex financial institution for MSME development. Manages Fund of Funds for Startups (FFS) on behalf of DPIIT.
india_note: SIDBI's FFS has committed ₹10,000 crore to 130+ AIFs which then invest in startups. Also provides direct soft loans via SMILE scheme.

definition: Serviceable Obtainable Market — the realistic share of SAM you can capture in the near term (3-5 years) given competition, sales capacity, and resources.
india_note: Investors want SOM to be defensible and growing. A 1% SOM of a ₹1,000 Cr SAM = ₹10 Cr revenue target — use this math in your pitch.

definition: Total Addressable Market — the maximum possible revenue opportunity for a product/service if 100% market penetration were achieved. Used in investor pitches.
india_note: Don't use global TAM for India-focused startups. Use India-specific data from IBEF, NASSCOM, Redseer, RedSeer to show credible India TAM.

definition: Non-binding document summarizing the key terms of a proposed investment — valuation, dilution, investor rights, and conditions. Binding only on exclusivity clause.
india_note: Indian term sheets have additional clauses: FEMA compliance conditions, RBI filings, ROF (right of first refusal), drag-along, tag-along rights.

definition: A startup valued at $1 billion or more in a private funding round. Coined by VC Aileen Lee in 2013.
india_note: India has 111 unicorns (2024). Most unicorns are in FinTech, EdTech, E-Commerce, and SaaS. Bengaluru leads with 40+ unicorn HQs.

definition: Udyam Registration — the mandatory MSME registration certificate issued by Government of India via udyamregistration.gov.in, replacing the old Udyog Aadhaar.
india_note: Udyam registration unlocks: priority sector lending, government procurement preference, CGTMSE credit guarantee, NSIC benefits. Takes 10 minutes online.

definition: Unified Payments Interface — India's real-time payment system enabling instant bank transfers using a VPA (Virtual Payment Address). Built by NPCI.
india_note: UPI processed ₹20 lakh crore/month (FY24). 600+ banks and 150+ apps on UPI. PhonePe (47%), Google Pay (36%), Paytm (8%) dominate.

definition: Revenue and cost associated with a single unit of business — one customer, one order, one transaction. Determines whether the business is fundamentally profitable at scale.
india_note: Investors want to see positive contribution margin per order/customer BEFORE Series A. Negative unit economics (buying customers at a loss) is unsustainable.

definition: The process by which founders and employees earn their equity over time according to a schedule. Prevents early departures with full equity.
india_note: Standard Indian startup vesting: 4-year vesting, 1-year cliff. Without vesting schedules, a departing co-founder retains all equity — a major risk.

definition: Short-term liquidity needed for day-to-day operations. Working capital = Current Assets - Current Liabilities.
india_note: Indian B2B startups often struggle with 60-90 day payment cycles from enterprise clients. SIDBI SMILE and CGTMSE help finance working capital gaps.

definition: Year over Year — comparison of a metric versus the same period in the previous year. Standard growth measurement to remove seasonality.
india_note: Indian startups report YoY growth in pitches. 100% YoY ARR growth is the minimum benchmark for a strong Series A pitch in India (2024 market).

definition: A startup valued at $10 billion or more in private markets. 10x a unicorn.
india_note: India decacorns: Byju's (peak $22B, now disputed), Nykaa (post-IPO $7.4B), PhonePe ($12B), Swiggy ($10.7B), Flipkart ($37B pre-Walmart).

definition: A funding round at a valuation lower than the previous round. Often results in significant dilution for existing shareholders and signals company underperformance.
india_note: Several high-profile Indian down rounds post-2021: Byju's, OYO, Meesho, GoTo. Often triggers anti-dilution provisions for earlier investors.

definition: A financial instrument giving the holder the right to purchase shares at a specified price before an expiry date. Often attached to debt instruments.
india_note: Warrants used in convertible note deals and in Compulsorily Convertible Preference Shares (CCPS) instruments in Indian startups.

definition: Compulsorily Convertible Preference Shares — the most common equity instrument used by VC investors in India. Converts to equity shares mandatorily upon IPO or agreed timeline.
india_note: Indian VCs almost universally invest via CCPS (not ordinary shares). CCPS carry preferential rights: liquidation preference, anti-dilution, drag-along.

definition: The 'Angel Tax' provision in Income Tax Act taxing share premium above FMV as income. Can be 30%+ effective tax on funding received by startups.
india_note: DPIIT-recognised startups exempted by filing Form 2. File Form 2 BEFORE your first external investment to avoid retrospective issues.

definition: Most Favoured Nation — a clause in convertible notes that entitles the holder to the best terms offered to any subsequent note holder in the same round.
india_note: Common in Indian angel rounds. If you offer better terms (lower cap, higher discount) to later investors, earlier MFN holders can claim those terms.

definition: Investor protection clause that adjusts conversion ratio downward (giving more shares) if a future round is at a lower valuation (down round).
india_note: Full ratchet anti-dilution (severe for founders) vs. broad-based weighted average (standard in Indian VC deals). Negotiate for weighted average.

definition: Form FC-GPR (Foreign Currency — General Permission Route) — mandatory RBI filing within 30 days of receiving foreign investment in an Indian company.
india_note: Filed by the Indian company through its AD (Authorised Dealer) bank on FIRMS portal. Penalty for late filing: ₹5,000-50,000 per year of delay.

definition: Investor right that allows majority shareholders to force minority shareholders to participate in a company sale on the same terms.
india_note: Standard in Indian VC term sheets. Typically triggered when 75%+ shareholders approve a sale. Protects investors' ability to exit even if founders resist.

definition: Minority shareholder right to join (tag along) any sale by majority shareholders under the same terms. Protects minority from being left behind.
india_note: Standard in Indian deals. Gives founders and ESOPs holders the right to sell if investors are selling — prevents founders being stuck.

definition: Related Party Transactions List — disclosure of transactions between the company and its promoters, directors, or their relatives. Required by SEBI for listed companies and in board resolutions.
india_note: Pre-IPO startups must clean up all RPTs. Common issue: founders paying personal expenses through company accounts.

definition: Revenue minus variable costs (COGS, delivery, payment processing fees). Shows profitability per transaction before fixed costs. Critical for unit economics validation.
india_note: Zomato was contribution margin positive before EBITDA positive. Zepto hit contribution margin positivity in 2024. This is the key investor checkpoint.
